label: The Caustic Loom
description: The Caustic Loom is a narrow prismatic shop cut into a shadow interval off the meridian glare corridors, its frontage formed from stepped glass ribs that break light into disciplined color bars. Interior walls carry latticed channels that trap refracted radiance and cast it as fixed-angle caustics across display plinths of dark crystalline stone. Shelving is built as rectilinear frames aligned to the city’s tensile geometry, presenting wares as calibrated facets rather than piled goods. A ceiling of laminar glass panes is scored with angle-marks used to read the meridian bands by color and incidence.
